video.title: Team Dominance Over The Cobbles! | E3 Saxo Bank Classic Highlights 2022

all_candidates.title: Team Dominance Over The Cobbles! | E3 Saxo Bank Classic Highlights 2022

transcript.clean_text: welcome along to highlights of the e3 saxo bank classic the official start of the cobble classic season and a race you can catch on demand over on gcn plus the 204 kilometer route includes many of the familiar and infamous bergs and cobblestone sectors from the tour of flanders amongst the 17 climbs the outer cloudsburg nocteburg aikenberg patterberg and outer claremont add in five flat sections of cobbles and there is almost no rest at all for the riders in this race a race that was fast from the start but it was ignited by jungle bismuth and weapon art once they hit the timeboard with 80 kilometers still to go that move took a very select group clear that included last year's winner casper aspring plus two of monarch's teammates teach bernout and christophe laporte they made short work of bridging the gap to what was left of the day's early breakaway behind a concerted chase from the ineos grenadiers brought the gap down enough to allow jonathan levious to attempt to bridge across and before the top of the aikenberg he had joined the front group on the road that group swelled over the following kilometers in the race until the group was around about 20 riders strong but it wouldn't be that long before it became much much smaller again jumbo bismuth took the front three positions on entry into the putterburg but note laying it all on the line for his teammates who would duly take full advantage van out stomping up the 20 graded with only la court able to go with him danielle gomez unable to follow but it was a brilliant ride from the eritrea and what was his first ever cobbled classic the gap back from the leading duo to the riders behind was already over 15 seconds by the time he hit the foot of the descent off the putterberg and over the top of the adaquara month within two climbs and 36 kilometers remaining it was already up to 20. the group behind did get themselves reasonably organized all rolling through and off over the top of the cobblestone climbs but they couldn't eat into the lead of the front two riders and bernard today looked like a machine out on the road of flanders as he has done in almost every day that he's raced so far in 2022. with 28 days to go their gap had risen further and 12 days later it was abundantly clear that this group was fighting out for the third place maharaj as queen van baal navis gumay kung and madwas resigned to their fate it's not often you get to ride away from a world-class field with your teammate in a professional bike race but this is the second time that these two have experienced that this season coming in towards alabeca and the finish the attack started in the following group with some less confident in their sprint than others they were still together as the leaders approached the flam rouge the only question that remained was who would be allowed to cross the finish line first but there really could only be one answer a belgian superstar a belgian national champion what bernard became just the second rider in the history of the e3 to win in that jersey 58 years after rick van lloyed in 1964 van art is on the form of his life behind nobody reacted when stefan kuhn made his move and the swiss man from group fdj had more than enough power in his legs to take third on the day now the rainbow winner took the spring for fourth just ahead of one of the most exciting young talents in the site and today binion gomez taking fifth at the age of just 21. nevaeh's madwes van barbernot and asgrain would round out the top 10 on the day well who can stop these two and this team it's going to be hard for anybody to match them but we'll find out over the next few days again wolverine comes on sunday dwarsdorf landed on wednesday and then a tour of flanders a week on sunday territory restrictions apply but we hope you can join us for them on gcn plus you
